positive.prompt: Explicitly run a bounded code review and fix loop against the approved spec and implementation plan
Review this completed implementation against docs/spec.md and docs/plan.md, make a repair plan for deviations, fix them, then have a fresh subagent re-review it
Use a review-fix workflow that decides whether findings need a plan change or a user decision before repairing the code

negative.prompt: Give this pull request a one-time read-only code quality review before I merge it; do not change any code
Break this approved specification into an ordered implementation plan before coding starts
Implement the approved plan now: make the requested code changes and run the targeted tests.
Continue with the next task in the implementation plan; apply the changes and verify them.

evals.prompt: Use the approved spec at docs/specs/notifications.md and plan at docs/plans/notifications.md to review the completed implementation. If the reviewer finds deviations, repair only safe local issues, verify them, and re-review up to three cycles.
evals.expected_output: A bounded, evidence-backed review-and-repair loop that preserves the approved contract and reaches a clean, blocked, stalled, or maximum-cycle terminal state
expectations: The workflow treats the supplied specification as the highest behavioral contract and the plan as an implementation-scope contract
A fresh read-only reviewer evaluates the full implementation diff against the supplied spec and plan rather than reviewing only the latest commit
Before writing a Repair Plan, the primary agent adjudicates whether each material finding is locally repairable, needs a plan or spec change, or requires a user decision
Each repair item maps to a spec or plan anchor, a minimal code change, an explicit non-goal, and a verification command
The workflow does not invoke planning-and-task-breakdown or silently redefine the approved spec or plan
After a repair it runs relevant verification and obtains a fresh independent re-review, with a bounded cycle count
The workflow reports a truthful terminal state instead of claiming success when it is blocked, stalled, awaiting a user decision, or out of cycles

evals.prompt: A code reviewer says the completed implementation violates AC-4, but the only two plausible fixes change a public API in incompatible ways and the current plan is ambiguous. Run the review-and-fix workflow.
evals.expected_output: A concise adjudication that pauses for a user decision rather than inventing a repair plan or changing the approved contract
expectations: The primary agent records the finding's contract anchor and the evidence for the claimed deviation
It identifies the material API compatibility trade-off and classifies the finding as requiring a user decision or plan change
It presents concrete options, a recommendation when evidence supports one, and the consequence of each option
It does not edit code, create a speculative Repair Plan, or launch planning-and-task-breakdown while user authority is required
The loop enters a truthful waiting or blocked terminal state that allows the user to respond
